name: nop
longDesc: Does nothing (No Operation). Used to pad the assembly code. Can be used to occupy functions for later ammendments.

name: ret
longDesc: Used to return function back to caller.

name: sync
longDesc:
Sync must be used when creating a thread on a looping function. Examples are functions that wait more than one frame for a condition to occur, such as a room being cleared, a switch being pressed, or a register being set. Sync should be called at least once in such loops. Not using sync inside of these loops may cause the game to freeze.

One sync equals one frame elapsed in game, and 30 frames equals one second. Sega sometimes used multiple syncs in a function to force a specific delay, such as displaying a window_msg some number of frames after zooming in the camera.

On more technical terms, sync will cause the current coroutine to yield, allowing other coroutines to run (thread actually creates a coroutine and not a thread, and they don't run parallelly). Special menus called through opcodes will yield on their own and do not require a sync inside their function.

Opcodes that will automatically yield:
ret
exit
window_msg
message
chat_box
disp_msg_qb
add_msg
list
fadein
fadeout
award_item_name
award_item_select
award_item_ok
get_item_id

name: thread
notes: found in q236 gc, Fix Lee
description: Create a new execution thread at the given function label.
longDesc:
Used to thread a constantly running function regardless of what floor you are on.
(To thread functions that will end when the players change floors see thread_stg)
Threads allow for the threaded function to run all while letting the main script continue on.
Usually used for looping functions that jmp back on their selves (time), or when you need to preform a constant check of a register.
When using threaded functions make sure you start the threaded function with a sync.

name: va_start
notes: Variable start, Use argments for variables.
longDesc: Initializes a variable argument list.

name: va_end
longDesc: Clears the variable argument list. This MUST be run after every va_start.

name: va_call

args.name: label
args.desc: Function label to call with pushed args

description: Calls the specified function and pushes the values to that function.
longDesc: Calls the specified function and pushes the values to that function.

name: let
longDesc: Used to set a register to the same value of another register

name: leti
description: Load immediate dword into register: `leti Rdest, VALUE`
longDesc: Used to set a register to specific integer value

name: letb
description: Load immediate byte into register: `letb Rdest, VALUE`
longDesc: Used to set a register to specific integer value that is stored in a single byte.

name: letw
description: Load immediate word into register: `letw Rdest, VALUE`
longDesc:
Used to set a register to specific integer value that is stored as a word.
(Name subject to change.)

name: set
longDesc: Used to set a register to a boolean value of 1 (true).

name: clear
longDesc: Used to set a register to a boolean value of 0 (false).

name: rev
longDesc: Used to toggle a register's boolean value.

name: gset
longDesc: These flags are used to store the current progress of a character in story mode quests. These generally aren't used with any quest other than the story mode quests.

name: gclear
longDesc: These flags are used to store the current progress of a character in story mode quests. These generally aren't used with any quest other than the story mode quests.

name: grev
longDesc: These flags are used to store the current progress of a character in story mode quests. These generally aren't used with any quest other than the story mode quests.  Sets the flag to whatever it wasn't, the equivalent of a logical NOT operation.

name: glet

args.name: flag
args.desc: Global flag index to set to 1

notes: Lee, found in q71 DC Ver.1

name: gget
longDesc: These flags are used to store the current progress of a character in story mode quests. These generally aren't used with any quest other than the story mode quests.

name: add
longDesc: Used to add the value of one register to another. For adding integers to registers see addi

name: addi
longDesc: Used to add a integer value to a register. For adding registers to registers see add

name: sub
longDesc: Used to subtract the value of one register from another. For subtracting integers from registers see subi

name: subi
longDesc: Used to subtract a integer value from a register. For subtracting registers from registers see sub

name: mul
longDesc: Used to multiply the value of one register by another. For multiplying registers by integers see muli

name: muli
longDesc: Used to multiply the value of one register by an integer. For multiplying registers by registers see mul

name: div
longDesc: Used to multiply the value of one register by another. For multiplying registers by integers see divi

name: divi
longDesc: Used to divide the value of one register by an integer. For dividing registers another register see div

name: and
longDesc: Used to perform a bitwise AND operation of one register with the value of another. For performing AND on registers with integers see andi

name: andi
longDesc: Used to perform a bitwise AND operation of one register with an integer value. For performing AND on registers with other registers see and

name: or
longDesc: Used to perform a bitwise OR operation of one register with the value of another. For performing OR on registers with integers see ori

name: ori
longDesc: Used to perform a bitwise OR operation of one register with an integer value. For performing OR on registers with other registers see or

name: xor
longDesc: Used to perform a bitwise XOR operation of one register with the value of another. For performing XOR on registers with integers see xori

name: xori
longDesc: Used to perform a bitwise XOR operation of one register with an integer value. For performing XOR on registers with other registers see xor

name: mod
longDesc: Used to perform modular math on one register with the value of another. For performing MOD on registers with integers see modi

name: modi
longDesc: Used to perform modular math on one register with a integer value. For performing MOD on registers with other registers see mod

name: jmp
longDesc: Used to jump to a function with no conditions.

name: call
longDesc: Used to call a function and then return to the function that called it.

args.name: label
args.desc: Label to jump to if any listed register is set

args.name: label
args.desc: Label to jump to if any listed register is clear

name: jmp_=

name: jmpi_=
description: Jump if Rn == immediate: `jmpi_= Rn, VALUE, LABEL`

args.desc: Register whose value selects the jump target

args.name: reg

args.desc: List of labels indexed by register value
